qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe Java deserialization in ObjectDecoder permits arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unsafe deserialization in ObjectDecoder (codec-base), which is unrelated to the advisory's HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood DoS in DefaultHttp2FrameReader (codec-http2). Different vulnerability type (deserialization RCE vs HTTP/2 DoS), different component, different root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes an HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame flood DoS vulnerability in Netty's DefaultHttp2FrameReader.verifyContinuationFrame() method, which fails to enforce a frame count limit and allows zero-byte frames to bypass maxHeaderListSize protection. The qualifying finding reports a completely different vulnerability (deserialization RCE in ObjectDecoder) in a different component (codec-base) that is unrelated to the HTTP/2 frame handling code identified in the advisory.
